description: GrpcProtocolHandler exists with full implementation (unary, streaming, serve, close) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler ABC. Method signatures (handle_outbound/service/method/payload, serve/handler) differ completely from ABC (prepare_outbound/payload/ctx, process_inbound/app_obj/ctx). Cannot verify protocol contract via IDE static analysis; LSP broken.
proposed_fix: Make GrpcProtocolHandler explicitly inherit IProtocolHandler; either rename methods to match ABC or update ABC to accommodate gRPC-specific signatures.

description: GraphQLProtocolHandler exists with full implementation (queries, subscriptions, error formatting) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler ABC. Method signatures (handle_inbound/raw_payload/context/executor, handle_outbound/query/variables/sender) differ entirely from ABC. Cannot verify contract via IDE static analysis.
proposed_fix: Make GraphQLProtocolHandler explicitly inherit IProtocolHandler; unify interface while preserving GraphQL-specific semantics (query/variables/operationName).

description: WebhookProtocolHandler exists with full implementation (HMAC-SHA256, idempotency, replay protection) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler ABC. Missing process_outbound_response() and close() methods. Resource leak: idempotency_store grows unbounded.
proposed_fix: Make WebhookProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response() and close(); add periodic idempotency_store cleanup.

description: LocalIpcProtocolHandler exists with full implementation (outbound, inbound, streaming, peer verification) but does NOT inherit IProtocolHandler ABC. Missing process_outbound_response() method. Type safety not guaranteed.
proposed_fix: Make LocalIpcProtocolHandler inherit IProtocolHandler; add process_outbound_response().

description: IProtocolHandler(ABC) defines 4 abstract methods (prepare_outbound, process_inbound, process_outbound_response, close) but NONE of the 7 handlers (http, cli, inprocess, grpc, graphql, webhook, local_ipc) inherit it. Contract is structural-only; IDE static analysis catches zero violations; LSP completely broken.
proposed_fix: Make all 7 handlers explicitly inherit IProtocolHandler; evaluate if ABC abstract methods need updating to accommodate natural protocol signature diversity.

description: ProtocolErrorMapper.map() defines ProtocolType.LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, WEBHOOK in the enum but has no dedicated _to_local_ipc(), _to_graphql(), _to_webhook() methods. The match statement (line 70-76) falls through to case _: return cls._to_http(cat). Local IPC errors appear as HTTP 500; GraphQL errors get HTTP headers; retryable hints are inaccurate.
column11: _to_webhook() methods to ProtocolErrorMapper; add retryable constants for each; update match statement to handle these protocol types explicitly rather than falling back to HTTP.

description: IProtocolHandler ABC assumes uniform prepare_outbound(payload, ctx) and process_inbound(app_obj, ctx) signatures, but each handler has fundamentally different parameters: http needs method/path/headers/payload; cli needs command/env/payload; graphql needs query/variables/operationName; inprocess needs target_override. The uniform contract is architecturally wrong for multi-protocol support. No handler can substitute another; ABC is currently useless as a contract; protocol_provider falsely returns IProtocolHandler types.
proposed_fix: Redesign IProtocolHandler: either (a) convert to ProtocolHandlerInterface with Generic Types per-protocol
column10: or (b) reduce ABC to only truly common methods (close) and let each handler define its own protocol-specific signatures freely without pretending uniformity.

description: 4 of 7 handlers are missing close() AND process_outbound_response(): InProcessProtocolHandler, CliProtocolHandler, GraphQLProtocolHandler, WebhookProtocolHandler. Incomplete lifecycle management causes resource leaks across the multi-protocol fabric. Only http, grpc, and local_ipc have close().
proposed_fix: Add close() (for resource release) and process_outbound_response() (for outbound response handling) to each of the 4 deficient handlers; provide default no-op implementations in IProtocolHandler ABC.

description: CliProtocolHandler opens stdin/stdout in handle_inbound (line 113) but has no close() to release them. WebhookProtocolHandler accumulates idempotency_store entries indefinitely (only clears at 100k entries via clear_expired_idempotency). Both leak resources in long-running applications.
proposed_fix: Add close() to CliProtocolHandler to release stdin/stdout handles. Improve WebhookProtocolHandler.clear_expired_idempotency() with TTL-based partial eviction instead of full clear at 100k threshold.

description: _RETRYABLE sets exist only for HTTP (line 49: {429,500,502,503,504}), CLI (line 50: {1,2}), and gRPC (line 51: {4,14}). LOCAL_IPC, GRAPHQL, and WEBHOOK have no retryable status lists. Even after adding _to_* methods (PBC-006), retryable flags for these protocols will be inaccurate, causing unnecessary retries or missed retry opportunities.
column11: and _WEBHOOK_RETRYABLE constants to ProtocolErrorMapper; document protocol-specific retry criteria for each; integrate into respective _to_* methods.
